author: bharath.vedartham
content:
<@586161934425128960> just want to clear the things you want to test here:
1. The IL commitee have selected some txs to be required in the payload but the builder doesn't include them. Here the block should be rejected by fork choice
2. The builder has not included some txs but the IL commitee has not yet selected those txs to be required in the payload i.e majority of the n/w doesn't have the txs in their IL store. Here, the block should not be rejected by fork choice but in the upcoming slots, the IL commitee will discover these public mempool txs and select them to be included in the block. Then the buildoor should include the txs in the block

author: bharath.vedartham
content: if we want to test the case where the buildoor ignores FOCIL txs, we can test that today. But if we want to test the case where the buildoor includes FOCIL txs, we have not implemented that yet today. we will need new payload attributes which includes the inclusion list txs

author: bharath.vedartham
content: and a new engine api where I can pass those inclusion list txs

author: jih2nn
content:
As we made great progress on Glamsterdam during the interop, it's perfect time to pipeline Hegota development. I'm happy to coordinate FOCIL (or Hegota) development again! We could use this time to find bugs in the spec, add tests and get up-to-date understanding of client implementation.

As the first step, I've just updated [Kurtosis config](https://github.com/jihoonsong/local-devnet-focil/blob/main/kurtosis/kurtosis.config.registry.interop.yaml). You can also find current client implementations [here](https://github.com/jihoonsong/focil-for-implementors/wiki/FOCIL-for-Implementors#client-implementations). We will going to have more tests for next few months to help stablize client implementations. <@510888004366368794> will help us on EEST and I will add CL tests.

If you have any questions or requests, let me know either here or in DM!

author: jih2nn
content:
As a debugging tip for who wasn't in FOCIL development a year ago, after entering Heze fork, you can click a slot number and check the IL tab on the right to see whether each IL transaction is included in the payload. Any missing IL transaction is shown with "no" label. But this naively checks whether the transaction is present; it doesn't consider whether a missing IL transaction is validly omitted.

This is enough until we have clients work on the very happy case, i.e., pull IL transactions only from the public mempool. I think we can focus on satisfying this very basic expectation for the time being.

author: ivanlitteri
content:
ethrex:local has the latest changes
```yaml
participants:
  - el_type: ethrex
    el_image: ethrex:local
    cl_type: lodestar
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:focil
    count: 2
  - el_type: ethrex
    el_image: ethrex:local
    el_extra_params:
      - "--mempool.private"
    cl_type: lodestar
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:focil
    count: 2
  - el_type: besu
    el_image: ethpandaops/besu:focil
    cl_type: lodestar
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:focil
    count: 2

network_params:
  preset: minimal
  gloas_fork_epoch: 1
  heze_fork_epoch: 2

spamoor_params:
  image: ethpandaops/spamoor:master
  spammers: []

additional_services:
  - dora
  - spamoor

dora_params:
  image: bbusa/dora:focil

port_publisher:
  additional_services:
    enabled: true
    public_port_start: 30000
```
